title: Rename app to Monet Tracker, update Daily Spending chart color, and polish UI

requirements.text: Change the color used by the Dashboard 'Daily Spending' graph (weekly bar chart) to a different chart color token so it is visually distinct from the other chart.

quotes: give a different colour to Daily spend graph

acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/charts/WeeklySpendBarChart.tsx`, the `<Bar ... />` fill no longer uses `hsl(var(--chart-1))` and instead uses a different existing chart token (e.g., `hsl(var(--chart-2))`).
The weekly bar chart remains readable in both light and dark themes (bars, axes, tooltip).
No other charts change color unless necessary to preserve visual contrast.

requirements.text: Rename the app to "Monet Tracker" everywhere the app name is presented to the user (header brand, footer copyright, Settings/About copy, and the HTML document title).

quotes: give name to this app Monet tracker

acceptanceCriteria: Header brand text in `frontend/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` displays "Monet Tracker" instead of "ExpenseTracker".
Footer copyright line in `frontend/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` references "Monet Tracker".
The Settings "About" description in `frontend/src/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx` references "Monet Tracker" (and no longer mentions "ExpenseTracker").
All user-facing occurrences of the old app name are removed from the frontend.

requirements.text: Update the UI styling to look more professional and cohesive across the app shell and top-level screens (without adding new features): refine spacing, typography hierarchy, and subtle surface styling to match a polished finance-tracker aesthetic.
acceptanceCriteria: The AppShell header (brand + nav) has improved visual polish (consistent alignment, spacing, and typographic hierarchy) while keeping existing navigation items and routes unchanged.
Cards and key sections on primary screens (Dashboard, Add Expense, Expense List, Settings) maintain consistent spacing and visual rhythm (e.g., section headers, card padding, grid gaps).
UI remains accessible: text contrast stays acceptable in both light and dark mode, and interactive elements retain clear hover/focus states.
No backend changes are introduced as part of this polish task.

constraints: Do not modify any files under `frontend/src/components/ui` (compose existing UI components instead).
Do not edit immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., `frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x)`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`).
Keep backend architecture unchanged (single Motoko actor); no backend work is required for these changes.
nonGoals: Adding new product features (budgets, recurring expenses, exports, payments, notifications).
Changing expense calculations, filters, or data model.
Implementing new authentication methods beyond Internet Identity.
